articles.title: US-Iran talks in Switzerland canceled. And, DHS to give police facial recognition app
articles.summary: The U.S.-Iran talks that were set to happen in Switzerland have been canceled. And, the Department of Homeland Security has plans to give some local police access to ICE facial recognition technology.
articles.titleZh: 美国与伊朗在瑞士举行的会谈取消。并且，国土安全部将向警方提供面部识别应用程序
articles.summaryZh: 原定在瑞士举行的美国与伊朗会谈已被取消。而且，国土安全部计划让一些当地警察使用 ICE 面部识别技术。

contentParagraphs.en: Good morning. You're reading the Up First newsletter. Subscribe here to get it delivered to your inbox, and listen to the Up First podcast for all the news you need to start your day.
contentParagraphs.zh: 早上好。您正在阅读 Up First 时事通讯。在此订阅，将其发送到您的收件箱，并收听 Up First 播客，了解开始新的一天所需的所有新闻。

contentParagraphs.en: Today's top stories
contentParagraphs.zh: 今日头条新闻

contentParagraphs.en: Vice President Vance is postponing his trip to Switzerland, where he was set to negotiate terms of a peace agreement with Iran. This week, President Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian signed a memorandum, which offers benefits to Iran but is only a first step toward a comprehensive deal. Yesterday, the U.S. Central Command announced that U.S. forces lifted their blockade on ships entering and exiting Iranian ports and coastal areas. This action is one of the conditions of a ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran, as both countries enter the next phase of negotiations over the next 60 days. Read the full text of Trump's preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement to end the war here.
contentParagraphs.zh: 副总统万斯推迟了前往瑞士的行程，他原定在那里与伊朗就和平协议的条款进行谈判。本周，特朗普总统和伊朗总统马苏德·佩泽什基安签署了一份备忘录，该备忘录为伊朗带来了好处，但这只是迈向全面协议的第一步。昨天，美国中央司令部宣布，美军解除了对进出伊朗港口和沿海地区的船只的封锁。这一行动是美国和伊朗达成停火协议的条件之一，因为两国将在未来 60 天内进入下一阶段的谈判。在此阅读特朗普结束战争的美伊初步协议全文。

contentParagraphs.en: In this picture obtained from Iran's ISNA news agency on Thursday, vessels are seen anchored in Bandar Abbas along the Strait of Hormuz.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在周四从伊朗 ISNA 通讯社获得的这张照片中，可以看到船只停泊在霍尔木兹海峡沿岸的阿巴斯港。

contentParagraphs.en: Amirhossein Khorgooei/ISNA/AFP via Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: Amirhossein Khorgooei/ISNA/法新社，盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: Amirhossein Khorgooei/ISNA/AFP via Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: Amirhossein Khorgooei/ISNA/法新社，盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: 🎧 Officials have not explained why the meeting has been delayed, but Israel's ongoing bombardment of southern Lebanon isn't helping matters, NPR's Rob Schmitz tells Up First. The first article of the agreement signed by Trump and Iran's president promises to ensure Lebanon's territorial integrity. Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has made it clear that Israeli forces plan to remain in southern Lebanon. Members of Netanyahu's cabinet have described this deal as "bad for Israel." For now, Iran's foreign ministry said that the signing ceremony is off and the White House said that plans for the upcoming technical talks have not yet been finalized. With both the U.S. and Iran threatening to strike each other, this agreement appears to be on very shaky ground, Schmitz says.🎧 Vance is the face of these negotiations, which means that if the U.S. does not achieve its objectives, he might bear a lot of the blame, NPR's Danielle Kurtzleben says. There were early signs of that this week when members of the right pinned their dissatisfaction with the deal on Vance. Kurtzleben adds that the vice president is not a seasoned diplomat, and it is hard to overstate how big a job negotiating this deal will be. Vance's past anti-interventionist positions may lend him some credibility in this role, and he's an aggressive messenger for the administration, Kurtzleben says. Sending the vice president to the negotiations also signals to Iran how significant this deal is to the U.S. Kurtzleben notes it is important to remember that this agreement is not the end of the war, but the start of what might be a challenging process toward that goal.
contentParagraphs.zh: 🎧 官员们尚未解释为何会议被推迟，但 NPR 的罗布·施密茨 (Rob Schmitz) 告诉 Up First，以色列对黎巴嫩南部的持续轰炸无济于事。特朗普和伊朗总统签署的协议第一条承诺确保黎巴嫩的领土完整。以色列总理本杰明·内塔尼亚胡明确表示，以色列军队计划留在黎巴嫩南部。内塔尼亚胡内阁成员称这项协议“对以色列不利”。目前，伊朗外交部表示签字仪式已经结束，白宫表示即将举行的技术会谈的计划尚未最终确定。施密茨说，由于美国和伊朗都威胁要互相打击，这项协议的基础似乎非常不稳定。 NPR 的丹妮尔·库茨莱本表示，万斯是这些谈判的代言人，这意味着如果美国没有实现其目标，他可能要承担很多责任。本周出现了早期迹象，右翼成员将对这笔交易的不满归咎于万斯。库尔茨莱本补充说，副总统并不是一位经验丰富的外交官，很难夸大谈判这项协议的工作量有多大。库茨莱本说，万斯过去的反干预主义立场可能会让他在这个角色上获得一些可信度，而且他是政府的一位积极进取的使者。派副总统参加谈判还向伊朗表明这项协议对美国有多么重要。库茨莱本指出，重要的是要记住，这项协议并不是战争的结束，而是实现这一目标的一个可能具有挑战性的进程的开始。

contentParagraphs.en: The grand opening ceremony of the Obama Presidential Center took place yesterday, featuring a star-studded guest list, musical performances and four former presidents, including President Obama himself. The event featured musical performances and appearances by Bruce Springsteen, Christina Aguilera, John Legend, Stevie Wonder and several other stars. The ceremony evoked the hope and change vibes of the Obama era. Every living former president attended the ceremony except one: Trump. During a press preview earlier this month, longtime Obama White House aide and current CEO of the Obama Foundation, Valerie Jarrett, said that Trump is welcome to visit the museum but was not invited to the dedication. Despite his absence, Trump remained a feature of the event, with subtle comments about the future of democracy and American ideals.
contentParagraphs.zh: 奥巴马总统中心盛大的开幕仪式于昨天举行，嘉宾名单星光熠熠，音乐表演，包括奥巴马总统本人在内的四位前总统出席。布鲁斯·斯普林斯汀、克里斯蒂娜·阿奎莱拉、约翰·传奇、史蒂夫·旺德和其他几位明星在此次活动中进行了音乐表演和亮相。仪式唤起了奥巴马时代的希望和变革氛围。除了特朗普之外，所有在世的前总统都出席了仪式。在本月早些时候的新闻预览中，奥巴马白宫长期助手、奥巴马基金会现任首席执行官瓦莱丽·贾勒特 (Valerie Jarrett) 表示，欢迎特朗普参观博物馆，但没有被邀请参加落成典礼。尽管缺席，特朗普仍然是此次活动的一大亮点，他对民主的未来和美国理想发表了微妙的评论。

contentParagraphs.en: A student-led group at Emory Law School has asked the Supreme Court to evaluate the judiciary's system for addressing misconduct among its own ranks. Unlike most other American workers, tens of thousands of federal court employees are not covered under landmark civil rights protections and cannot seek assistance from an independent agency if they face harassment or discrimination at work. This case centers on the court's unique system for policing itself and whether it ensures that workers receive due process and equal protection under the law.
contentParagraphs.zh: 埃默里法学院的一个学生领导的团体要求最高法院评估司法系统处理其内部不当行为的制度。与大多数其他美国工人不同，数以万计的联邦法院雇员不受具有里程碑意义的民权保护，如果他们在工作中面临骚扰或歧视，也无法向独立机构寻求帮助。本案的焦点是法院独特的自我监管制度，以及它是否确保工人在法律下获得正当程序和平等保护。

contentParagraphs.en: A newly revealed document from the Department of Homeland Security outlines plans to equip local police working on its behalf with facial recognition technology to identify immigrants in the field. The tool involved is a mobile app called the ICE Task Force Module, which enables local police to scan the faces of individuals they stop in their communities. The app then compares the facial scans against more than 250 million government records, including those from the State Department's Visa records and the Traveler Verification Service. After scanning a person's face, the app directs the officer to either "not detain or arrest" the individual or to use a reference code to retrieve additional information from Immigration and Customs Enforcement.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国国土安全部最新披露的一份文件概述了为代表其工作的当地警察配备面部识别技术以识别现场移民的计划。所涉及的工具是一个名为 ICE 特别工作组模块的移动应用程序，该应用程序使当地警察能够扫描他们在社区中拦截的个人的面部。然后，该应用程序将面部扫描与超过 2.5 亿条政府记录进行比较，其中包括来自国务院签证记录和旅行者验证服务的记录。扫描一个人的面部后，该应用程序会指示官员“不拘留或逮捕”该人，或者使用参考码从移民和海关执法局检索更多信息。

contentParagraphs.en: Jalen Brunson and Rick Brunson of The New York Knicks celebrate winning the 2026 NBA Championship with a ticker tape parade at City Hall.
contentParagraphs.zh: 纽约尼克斯队的贾伦·布伦森和里克·布伦森在市政厅举行彩带游行，庆祝赢得 2026 年 NBA 总冠军。

contentParagraphs.en: Elias Wlliams for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的埃利亚斯·威廉姆斯

contentParagraphs.en: Elias Wlliams for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的埃利亚斯·威廉姆斯

contentParagraphs.en: New York City buzzed with excitement yesterday as fans flooded the streets to celebrate the Knicks' first NBA championship win since 1973. The ticker tape parade kicked off in Battery Park, traveled down Manhattan's famed "Canyon of Heroes" and ended at City Hall. There, New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani delivered a speech, showering praise on the Knicks team. Take a look at the photos from the event.
contentParagraphs.zh: 昨天，纽约市一片兴奋，球迷们涌上街头庆祝尼克斯队自 1973 年以来首次夺得 NBA 冠军。游行队伍从炮台公园开始，沿着曼哈顿著名的“英雄峡谷”行进，最后在市政厅结束。纽约市市长佐兰·马姆达尼 (Zohran Mamdani) 发表讲话，对尼克斯队大加赞扬。看看活动的照片。

contentParagraphs.en: Hugh Jackman as Robin Hood.
contentParagraphs.zh: 休·杰克曼饰演罗宾汉。

articles.title: GTA 6 - all you need to know about Rockstar's blockbuster game
articles.summary: Rockstar's sixth game in the franchise is set to be the biggest game release of the year.
articles.titleZh: GTA 6 - 关于 Rockstar 的热门游戏您需要了解的一切
articles.summaryZh: Rockstar 系列的第六款游戏将成为今年发行量最大的游戏。

contentParagraphs.en: GTA 6 - all you need to know about Rockstar's blockbuster game36 minutes agoLaura CressandLiv McMahon,Technology reportersRockstar GamesGTA 6 will feature Jason and Lucia as playable charactersThe latest instalment in Rockstar's blockbuster game franchise, Grand Theft Auto, is set to be the biggest games launch of the year.Analysts believe Rockstar's action adventure could become the most expensive game ever made, with estimates putting development costs at more than $1bn (£866m).We're still awaiting some crucial information about the game - but here's what we do and don't know about GTA 6 so far.When is GTA 6 coming out?GTA 6 will be released on 19 November - and with Rockstar recently opening up pre-orders, this date seems all but certain.In a statement when the second delay was announced, Rockstar said it needed extra months to finish the game with the level of polish fans had come to "expect and deserve".How much will GTA 6 cost and what can I play it on?The sixth game in the main series will be released on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X and S.There's no news on when a PC version will be released - it's also currently unknown whether it will come to Nintendo Switch 2.Rockstar and its parent company Take-Two has still not revealed its price. It's something that will likely only be revealed when pre-sales begin in late June. Some analysts have estimated the game could be the first to be priced at $100 (£76).What is GTA 6 about, where is it set and who are its main characters?GTA 6 will feature its first ever playable female protagonist in a 3D setting - Lucia - alongside her partner in life and crime, Jason, as a second playable character. Like other GTA games before it, the sixth chapter is set in a fictional US state - this time in Leonida, which is Rockstar's version of Florida.Fans of previous games will be excited to hear that Vice City, which is inspired by Miami, will return, featuring as the main city within the state.Where can I pre-order it?Rockstar has said GTA 6 will be available to pre-order on Thursday 25 June, both on digital stores and "other select retailers".Because it will be coming to the PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X/S initially, it can be added to people's wishlists on the PlayStation and Xbox e-stores.Rockstar GamesGTA 6's cover art features the game's protagonists Lucia and JasonHow long has Rockstar taken to make GTA 6?It's no secret that GTA 6 has been a few years in the making.The franchise's last instalment, GTA 5, was released in September 2013 - and quickly became one of the best-selling games of all time.It's unclear whether Rockstar has been working on the sixth instalment for all of that time, however, as in 2018 it also released another huge sequel - Red Dead Redemption 2.Rockstar confirmed for the first time that it was working on a sixth game in the series in February 2022.The rising cost of development and immense pressure to live up to the series' hype are all contributing factors as to why gamers have had to wait so long for the game's release.Why is there so much excitement about it?To put it simply, the GTA franchise is one of the biggest and most profitable entertainment properties in history.GTA 5 has sold nearly 230 million copies, making it one of the best-selling games of all time - which has generated billions of dollars in revenue for Rockstar.The game's sandbox gameplay, where players can explore vast open worlds with considerable freedom - sometimes controversially - has seen it be continuously lauded as the ultimate expression for what the interactive medium can do. Freelance video games journalist Vic Hood said each entry in the series "continued to push technical and gameplay boundaries", with GTA Online helping to "pioneer the live service model as we know it".She added that the games were also "extremely culturally relevant" too."Rockstar has always had its finger on the pulse of music, entertainment, societal, political, celebrity, and online culture trends, and they've never shied away from satirising these things in GTA games," she said.Speculation as to how a sixth game can top even the gigantic heights of its predecessor has meant that any latest news, no matter how small, has been met with a flurry of hype and excitement.Both of GTA 6's previous gameplay trailers currently have a total of roughly 447 million views combined.
contentParagraphs.zh: GTA 6 - 关于 Rockstar 重磅游戏您需要了解的一切 36 分钟前劳拉·克雷森 (Laura Cress) 和丽芙·麦克马洪 (Liv McMahon)，科技记者Rockstar GamesGTA 6 将以 Jason 和 Lucia 作为可玩角色 Rockstar 重磅游戏系列的最新作品《侠盗猎车手》将成为今年推出的最大规模的游戏。 分析人士认为，Rockstar 的动作冒险游戏可能会成为有史以来最昂贵的游戏，预计开发成本将超过10 亿美元（8.66 亿英镑）。我们仍在等待有关游戏的一些重要信息 - 但到目前为止，我们对 GTA 6 的了解和未知情况如下。GTA 6 何时发布？GTA 6 将于 11 月 19 日发布 - 随着 Rockstar 最近开放预订，这个日期似乎几乎确定。在宣布第二次延迟时的一份声明中，Rockstar 表示，根据波兰粉丝的“预期水平”，需要额外几个月的时间才能完成游戏GTA 6 的售价是多少，我可以在什么设备上玩？主系列的第六款游戏将在 PlayStation 5 和 Xbox Series X 和 S 上发布。PC 版本何时发布还没有消息，目前也不清楚它是否会登陆 Nintendo Switch 2。Rockstar 及其母公司 Take-Two 尚未透露其价格。这可能只有在六月下旬预售开始时才会揭晓。一些分析师估计这款游戏可能是第一款定价为 100 美元（76 英镑）的游戏。《GTA 6》讲述了什么，故事发生在哪里，主要角色是谁？《GTA 6》将在 3D 环境中推出有史以来第一个可玩的女性主角 - 露西亚 (Lucia)，以及她生活和犯罪中的伴侣杰森 (Jason)，作为第二个可玩角色。与之前的其他 GTA 游戏一样，第六章的背景设定在一个虚构的美国州 - 这次是在列奥尼达，这是 Rockstar 版的佛罗里达州。之前游戏的粉丝们会很高兴听到受迈阿密启发的《罪恶都市》将回归，并以该州的主要城市为特色。我可以在哪里预订它？Rockstar 表示，GTA 6 将于 6 月 25 日星期四在数字商店和“其他精选零售商”上提供预订。因为它即将推出最初，它可以添加到 PlayStation 5 和 Xbox Series X/S 上，可以添加到 PlayStation 和 Xbox 电子商店上人们的愿望清单中。Rockstar GamesGTA 6 的封面艺术以游戏主角露西娅和杰森为特色。Rockstar 制作 GTA 6 花了多长时间？众所周知，GTA 6 已经制作了几年。该系列的最后一部《GTA 5》于 2013 年 9 月发行 - 并且很快就成为有史以来最畅销的游戏之一。目前尚不清楚 Rockstar 是否一直在开发第六部作品，不过，在 2018 年，它还发布了另一部大型续作——《荒野大镖客：救赎 2》。Rockstar 于 2022 年 2 月首次确认正在开发该系列的第六部游戏。不断上升的开发成本和不辜负该系列宣传的巨大压力都是导致玩家等待如此之久的因素。游戏的发布为何如此令人兴奋？简单来说，GTA 系列是历史上规模最大、利润最高的娱乐产品之一。GTA 5 已售出近 2.3 亿份，成为有史以来最畅销的游戏之一，为 Rockstar 带来了数十亿美元的收入。该游戏的沙盒游戏玩法让玩家可以相当自由地探索广阔的开放世界（有时会引起争议），因此一直被誉为互动媒体的终极表达。可以做。自由视频游戏记者维克·胡德 (Vic Hood) 表示，该系列中的每一款作品都“不断突破技术和游戏玩法的界限”，其中《GTA 在线》帮助“开创了我们所知的实时服务模式”。她补充说，这些游戏也“与文化极其相关”。“Rockstar 一直紧扣音乐、娱乐、社会、政治、名人和在线文化趋势的脉搏，他们从不回避在 GTA 游戏中讽刺这些东西，”她关于第六款游戏如何超越前作的巨大高度的猜测意味着任何最新消息，无论多么小，都会引起一阵炒作和兴奋。《GTA 6》之前的两个游戏预告片目前总共有大约 4.47 亿次观看。

articles.title: Plans to end gazumping with binding agreements in house sales shake-up
articles.summary: Sales agreements will be legally binding sooner and making sellers provide more home information up front are part of the planned changes.

articles.title: Trump hints at new approach to North Korea’s nuclear programme
articles.summary: South Korea's president said Trump had told him the 'time had come' to focus on North Korea 'issue' at the G7 summit.
articles.titleZh: 特朗普暗示将对朝鲜核计划采取新方法
articles.summaryZh: 韩国总统表示，特朗普告诉他，在七国集团峰会上关注朝鲜“问题”的“时机已经到来”。

contentParagraphs.en: South Korea’s president said his US counterpart had told him the ‘time had come to pay attention to the North Korea issue’ at the G7 summit.United States President Donald Trump intends to shift his focus to North Korea’s nuclear programme now that Washington has reached an agreement with Iran, South Korea’s president has said.Lee Jae Myung said in a news conference that Trump told him on Friday at a G7 dinner that “the time had come to pay attention to the North Korea issue,” a comment that could signal renewed US focus on Pyongyang’s nuclear capabilities.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4Starbucks Korea to shut stores for history training after ‘Tank Day’ furorelist 2 of 4G7 meeting in France: What’s on agenda, who is attending?list 3 of 4Oil prices fall, stocks rally as US, Iran sign framework to end warlist 4 of 4Oil prices rise as Lebanon fighting erupts and Hormuz traffic still slowend of listLee also told Trump that sanctions against North Korea were “ineffective”, pointing to deepening military cooperation between Pyongyang and Moscow.“Even a small amount of assistance from Russia is of great help to North Korea,” Lee said.The two Koreas remain technically at war because their 1950-53 conflict ended in an armistice, not a peace treaty, and are separated by a Demilitarized Zone through which the border runs. North Korea announced its first nuclear test in 2006 and is believed to have dozens of nuclear weapons.Pyongyang’s foreign outreachKim has recently sought to enhance his standing with his allies, sending troops and munitions to aid Russia’s war against Ukraine.He also recently hosted China’s President Xi Jinping in Pyongyang, soon after Xi had held back-to-back summits in Beijing with Trump and Russian leader Vladimir Putin.Neither Pyongyang’s nor Beijing’s official statements mentioned the issue of North Korean denuclearisation – an outcome experts interpreted as tacit acceptance from China.Pyongyang has repeatedly declared itself an “irreversible” nuclear state since a 2019 summit between Kim and Trump, in Hanoi, collapsed over the scope of denuclearisation and sanctions relief.Kim has pledged to expand North Korea’s nuclear capabilities as he unveiled a new facility for developing nuclear bomb fuels.Trump met Kim three times during his first term – once declaring they were “in love” – as he pushed to hammer out a long-coveted deal on denuclearisation. But no tangible progress has been made.Trump stepped up his courtship of Kim during a tour of Asia last year, saying he was “100 percent” open to a meeting. The offer has gone unanswered.The US president even bucked decades of US policy by stating North Korea was “sort of a nuclear power”.On Sunday, Trump posted an uncaptioned photo of himself and North Korean leader Kim Jong Un taken at a meeting in Singapore in 2018.
contentParagraphs.zh: 韩国总统表示，美国总统在七国集团峰会上告诉他“是时候关注朝鲜问题了”。韩国总统表示，既然华盛顿已经与伊朗达成协议，美国总统唐纳德·特朗普打算将注意力转向朝鲜核计划。李在明在新闻发布会上表示，特朗普在周五的七国集团晚宴上告诉他，“现在是关注朝鲜问题的时候了”，这一评论可能表明美国将重新关注朝鲜核计划。平壤的核能力。推荐故事清单 4 项清单 1 之 4 星巴克韩国将在“坦克日”后关闭商店进行历史培训 法国 4G7 会议的狂暴者 2：议程是什么，谁参加？清单 3 之 4 石油价格下跌，股市上涨，美国、伊朗签署结束战争的框架 4 之 4 黎巴嫩战斗爆发和霍尔木兹交通仍然放缓，石油价格上涨 李还告诉特朗普李说，对朝鲜的制裁“无效”，并指出平壤和莫斯科之间正在深化军事合作。“即使是俄罗斯的少量援助，对朝鲜也有很大帮助。”从技术上讲，朝韩两国仍处于战争状态，因为两国1950-53年的冲突以停战协议而非和平条约结束，而且两国之间有一个非军事区，边界贯穿其中。朝鲜于 2006 年宣布进行首次核试验，据信拥有数十枚核武器。平壤的对外活动金正恩最近寻求提高自己在盟友中的地位，派遣军队和弹药援助俄罗斯对乌克兰的战争。不久前，习近平在北京与特朗普和俄罗斯领导人普京连续举行峰会后不久，他还在平壤接待了中国国家主席习近平。平壤和北京都没有发表官方声明提到朝鲜无核化问题——专家们将这一结果解读为中国的默许。自2019年金正恩和特朗普在河内举行的峰会因无核化和制裁解除的范围破裂以来，平壤一再宣称自己是“不可逆转”的核国家。金正恩承诺扩大朝鲜的核能力，并公布了开发核弹燃料的新设施。特朗普在第一个任期内曾三次会见金正恩，其中一次宣称他们“相爱” ——他推动敲定一项令人垂涎已久的无核化协议。但并未取得任何实质性进展。特朗普去年在亚洲之行期间加强了对金正恩的求爱，称他“100%”对会面持开放态度。这一提议没有得到回应。这位美国总统甚至违背了美国几十年来的政策，称朝鲜是“某种程度上的核国家”。周日，特朗普发布了一张他自己和朝鲜领导人金正恩 2018 年在新加坡会面时拍摄的无标题照片。

articles.title: UK's top data and AI regulator quits after 'inappropriate' humour
articles.summary: Edwards has been the boss of the data watchdog since January 2022, and said he had made inappropriate attempts at humour.
articles.titleZh: 英国最高数据和人工智能监管机构因“不当”幽默而辞职
articles.summaryZh: 爱德华兹自 2022 年 1 月起一直担任数据监管机构的老板，并表示他在幽默方面做出了不恰当的尝试。

contentParagraphs.en: UK's top data and AI regulator quits after 'inappropriate' humour39 minutes agoLiv McMahonTechnology reporterBBCJohn Edwards had been the information commissioner since 2022John Edwards, the UK's information commissioner, has resigned following a workplace investigation."I have accepted that there have been occasions where I exercised poor judgement and made attempts at humour that were inappropriate and caused offence," he said in a statement on Friday.The Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) is responsible for regulating AI in the UK and also oversees data protection regulation and the freedom of information law.Edwards' resignation was confirmed by the government, which said it had come after an independent probe "that took place regarding allegations made against him"."The government expects the highest standards of conduct from all senior leaders in public life," said a spokesperson for the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT)."Mr Edwards has acknowledged that his conduct fell below these standards."They added that existing legal arrangements mean deputy commissioners must undertake the regulator's responsibilities and "ensure continuity of leadership".The BBC has approached the ICO for comment.Edwards, who had been at the watchdog since January 2022, had already stepped back from his post in February when the investigation started.In his statement, shared on his LinkedIn page, he said while he disagreed with how the ICO's investigation had been carried out, "I accept that my position has become untenable".He said he did not want to become "a distraction" from the ICO's work and had notified the government of his resignation as both commissioner and chair of the ICO "effective immediately".The ICO said in a statement on 10 June its investigation had been completed and, finding "there is a case to answer", said the commissioner would be "temporarily unable to act in fulfilling his responsibilities for the remainder of the process".Increased scrutinyEdwards' resignation comes amid increased scrutiny over the ICO's work, particularly in dealing with data protection complaints from the public.Non-profit campaign groups the Good Law Project and the Open Rights Group (ORG) recently launched action to challenge the watchdog - accusing it of "brushing aside thousands of public data complaints"."John Edwards' departure is a chance for the Government to appoint a regulator with teeth, and reset the regulators' approach of providing data protection in name only," ORG executive director Jim Killock said on Friday."Parliament must ensure that the future Commission is run by professionals who want the law enforced, including against government data failures."Edwards said in his statement on Friday he was "proud" of his own contributions and that of ICO staff, more broadly."While I will no longer be able to continue this work in my current role, my commitment to the principles, values, and objectives that have guided my professional life remains unchanged," he added.The ICO is tasked with ensuring data and information rights are upheld in the UK.This includes making sure organisations are correctly handling people's data, and investigating potential breaches of the law.It has the power to take enforcement action against firms which do not comply.In serious cases, it can fine firms up to £17.5m, or 4% of their worldwide turnover in the previous financial year - whichever is higher.The ICO recently dished out a £14m fine to online platform Reddit, finding it had unlawfully used children's personal information and failed to adequately check the age of its users.
contentParagraphs.zh: 英国最高数据和人工智能监管机构因“不当”幽默而辞职 39分钟前 丽芙·麦克马洪 (Liv McMahon) 科技记者 BBC 科技记者 约翰·爱德华兹 (John Edwards) 自 2022 年起一直担任信息专员 英国信息专员约翰·爱德华兹 (John Edwards) 在工作场所调查后辞职。负责监管英国的人工智能，还负责监督数据保护监管和信息自由法。爱德华兹的辞职得到了政府的确认，政府表示，这是在“针对他的指控进行独立调查”之后做出的。科学、创新和技术部（DSIT）发言人表示，“政府期望所有高级领导人在公共生活中遵守最高的行为标准。爱德华兹先生承认他的行为低于这些标准。”他们补充说，现有的法律安排意味着副专员必须遵守这些标准。承担监管机构的责任并“确保领导的连续性”。BBC 已向 ICO 寻求评论。自 2022 年 1 月起一直担任监管机构的爱德华兹在调查开始时已于 2 月份辞去职务。在他的 LinkedIn 页面上分享的声明中，他表示，虽然他不同意 ICO 的调查方式，但“我承认我的立场已变得站不住脚”。他表示，他不想成为“分心” ICO 的工作，并已通知政府他辞去了 ICO 专员和主席的职务，“立即生效”。ICO 在 6 月 10 日的一份声明中表示，其调查已经完成，并发现“有一个案件需要答复”，并表示专员将“暂时无法履行其在剩余程序中的职责”。 ICO 的工作，特别是在处理公众的数据保护投诉方面。非营利组织 Good Law Project 和 Open Rights Group (ORG) 最近发起了挑战监管机构的行动，指责其“无视数以千计的公共数据投诉”。ORG 执行董事 Jim Killock 周五表示，“约翰·爱德华兹 (John Edwards) 的离职是政府任命一位强有力的监管者的机会，并重新调整监管机构名义上提供数据保护的做法。”议会必须确保未来的委员会爱德华兹在周五的声明中表示，他对自己以及更广泛的 ICO 工作人员的贡献感到“自豪”。“虽然我无法再以目前的职位继续这项工作，但我对指导我职业生涯的原则、价值观和目标的承诺保持不变。”他补充说。ICO 的任务是确保数据和信息权利在英国得到维护。这包括确保组织正确处理它有权对不遵守规定的公司采取执法行动。在严重的情况下，它可以对公司处以最高 1750 万英镑的罚款，或上一财年全球营业额的 4%（以较高者为准）。ICO 最近向在线平台 Reddit 处以 1400 万英镑的罚款，发现该平台非法使用儿童的个人信息，并且未能充分检查其用户的年龄。

articles.title: Warning over 'fragile' public finances as borrowing rises
articles.summary: The UK borrowed £23.3bn in May, according to official figures, up almost a third on the same month last year.
articles.titleZh: 随着借贷增加，公共财政“脆弱”警告
articles.summaryZh: 根据官方数据，英国 5 月份借款 233 亿英镑，比去年同月增加近三分之一。

articles.title: World Cup: Will Pulisic play against Australia and can USMNT qualify?
articles.summary: Will Christian Pulisic be fit for United States as they seek to secure top spot in Group D with win over Australia?
articles.titleZh: 世界杯：普利西奇会对阵澳大利亚吗？美国国家队能否晋级？
articles.summaryZh: 克里斯蒂安·普利西奇能否适合美国队，因为他们希望通过战胜澳大利亚队来确保 D 组头名的位置？

contentParagraphs.en: We might have only played one round of games in Group D, but the door to the World Cup 2026 knockout stages is open for both the United States and Australia, who meet in their second match in Seattle on Friday (19:00 GMT).After thumping Paraguay 4-1 in their opening game, the USMNT can secure qualification, and potentially even win the group with a match to spare, should they overcome the Socceroos on Friday.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4Iran to lodge complaint with FIFA over World Cup travel restrictionslist 2 of 4Bouaddi: Morocco’s teenage World Cup star who could have played for Francelist 3 of 4World Cup Day 9: USA, Brazil and Morocco return to actionlist 4 of 4Morocco’s World Cup captain Achraf Hakimi to stand trial on rape chargeend of listHowever, the same is true of Australia, who shocked Turkiye 2-0 with a counterattacking masterclass in their opening match.It all depends on the outcome of the showdown in Seattle and then the result of the game between Paraguay and Turkiye in Santa Clara a few hours later (03:00 GMT).Christian Pulisic has had his left calf wrapped in a sleeve during training sessions at the University of Washington [Kiyoshi Mio/Reuters]Is Christian Pulisic playing against Australia?USMNT talisman Christian Pulisic picked up an injury to his left calf in training ahead of the opening game against Paraguay, though he started the game in Los Angeles last Friday.He was the architect of the opening goal after seven minutes when he nipped between two defenders and found Weston McKennie, whose cross was turned into his own net by Paraguay’s Damian Bobadilla.Pulisic then laid on the second with a pass to Folarin Balogun just after the half-hour mark, though with USA 3-0 up at half-time, Pulisic did not reappear after the interval, replaced by Sebastian Berhalter.The 27-year-old AC Milan playmaker joined the team huddle before training on Thursday with his calf still strapped but did not take part in the session, instead heading to the gym with backroom staff.USMNT coach Mauricio Pochettino told his pre-match media conference: “He’s evolving. He’s much better from Friday. We’ll see. If we see he’s not available, he will be available for the next game [against Turkiye in Los Angeles on June 26 02:00 GMT].”The Argentinian added: “I think he’s doing a massive effort trying to be ready. I think for every single player that love their country it’s an amazing opportunity to enjoy and to help the team to perform and to win games.“When this type of thing happens (it’s) always painful, but I think Christian is strong and with great mentality and is doing a fantastic effort to try to be ready as soon as possible,” Pochettino said.Pulisic’s teammate McKennie told reporters: “Hopefully he can be there. I know he really wants to be and he’s doing everything that he can and the staff is doing everything that they can as well.”Giovanni Reyna celebrates after his goal in stoppage time against Paraguay in their World Cup 2026 opener [Kirby Lee/Imagn Images]Who can replace Pulisic?If Pulisic is ruled out, Pochettino has plenty of options to replace him.Balogun is sure to lead the line after his double against Paraguay, the first by a USMNT player at a World Cup since Bert Patenaude became the first-ever player to score a World Cup hat-trick 96 years ago, also against Paraguay.The three-goal margin of victory matched their biggest at a World Cup, also set in 1930, when the US finished third in the inaugural edition.Giovanni Reyna came on for the closing stages against Paraguay and scored a lovely trivela to complete the 4-1 victory in stoppage time – but he has not played more than 60 minutes in a match for German club Borussia Monchengladbach or his country since last December.Timothy Weah also made an impact after replacing Sergino Dest with 18 minutes remaining and he could potentially be inserted on the right or as support for Balogun through the middle, which might require McKennie to switch to the left of the attack.While he lacks the same X-factor, Berhalter replaced Pulisic against Paraguay and adds the set-piece capabilities which the US would miss in Pulisic’s absence, while Leeds United’s industrious winger Brenden Aaronson or Club America’s Alejandro Zendejas are alternative options after remaining on the bench last Friday, though neither has been a favourite for Pochettino.“We are evaluating all the possibilities, just in case, and then we will decide when we have the confirmation in one or another direction,” Pochettino said.Can USMNT qualify for the knockout stage on Friday?Yes, in fact they can even seal top spot in Group D.Should the United States beat Australia then they are guaranteed a top-two entry in the Round of 32 with six points.However, a win, coupled with a draw in the later game between Turkiye and Paraguay, or a Paraguay win, would ensure first place in Group D.Group stage finishing positions are determined by points initially, then head-to-head results between teams finishing level on points, with goal difference only used to separate teams which drew against one another.The winners of Group D will play in the San Francisco Bay Area Stadium on July 1 against a side finishing third in Groups B, E, F, I or J – potentially Bosnia, Ecuador, Sweden, Norway or Algeria.Who is Nestory Irankunda?History was made in the opening round in Vancouver as Irankunda blazed away before applying a cool finish after 27 minutes to become Australia’s youngest ever World Cup scorer.The 20-year-old – part of the youngest XI to start a World Cup match since France in 2018 – also became the first overseas-born Australian to find the net on the biggest stage.After his parents fled the civil war in their native Burundi to a Tanzanian refugee camp, Irankunda was born there in 2006 before moving to Australia as an infant.He came through the youth set up at A-League side Adelaide United and after scoring 16 goals and registering eight assists for the first team, earned a sur
contentParagraphs.zh: 我们在 D 组可能只打了一轮比赛，但 2026 年世界杯淘汰赛的大门已经向美国队和澳大利亚队敞开，美国队和澳大利亚队将于周五（格林威治标准时间 19:00）在西雅图进行第二场比赛。在首场比赛中以 4-1 击败巴拉圭队后，美国国家队可以确保出线资格，如果周五战胜澳大利亚队，甚至有可能在剩下一场比赛的情况下赢得小组赛胜利。 推荐故事列表 4 项列表 1 4伊朗就世界杯旅行限制向国际足联提出投诉list 2 of 4布阿迪：摩洛哥的世界杯少年明星，本可以为法国队效力list 3 of 4世界杯第9天：美国、巴西和摩洛哥重返赛场list 4 of 4摩洛哥世界杯队长阿什拉夫·哈基米因强奸指控接受审判end of list然而，澳大利亚也是如此，澳大利亚队以一场大师级反击以2-0震惊了土耳其这一切都取决于西雅图对决的结果，以及几个小时后巴拉圭和土耳其在圣克拉拉的比赛结果（格林威治标准时间 03:00）。克里斯蒂安·普利西奇在华盛顿大学的训练期间用袖子包裹着左小腿 [Kiyoshi Mio/Reuters]克里斯蒂安·普利西奇是否正在对阵澳大利亚？美国国家队护身符克里斯蒂安·普利西奇在接下来的训练中左小腿受伤上周五对阵巴拉圭的首场比赛，他在洛杉矶首发。七分钟后，他从两名后卫中间夹过，找到了韦斯顿·麦肯尼，后者的传中被巴拉圭队的达米安·博巴迪拉变成了自家球门。半小时后，普利西奇将球传给了弗拉林·巴洛贡，但在半场结束时，美国队以 3-0 领先，普利西奇在第二场比赛中没有再次登场。中场休息时，由塞巴斯蒂安·贝哈尔特替换。这位27岁的AC米兰组织核心在周四训练前加入了球队，小腿仍然绑着，但没有参加训练，而是与后台工作人员一起前往健身房。USMNT教练毛里西奥·波切蒂诺在赛前媒体发布会上说：“他正在进步。从周五开始他好多了。我们拭目以待。如果我们发现他无法上场，他将可以参加下一场比赛[格林尼治标准时间 6 月 26 日 02:00 在洛杉矶对阵图尔基耶]。”阿根廷人补充道：“我认为他正在付出巨大的努力来做好准备，我认为对于每一个热爱自己国家的球员来说，这是一个享受并帮助球队表现并赢得比赛的绝佳机会。“当这种事情发生时（它）总是令人痛苦的，但我认为克里斯蒂安很坚强，心态很好，并且正在尽最大努力尽快做好准备。波切蒂诺说：“有可能。”普利西奇的队友麦肯尼告诉记者：“希望他能在那里。我知道他真的很想成为这样的人，他正在尽其所能，工作人员也正在尽其所能。”乔瓦尼·雷纳在 2026 年世界杯揭幕战对阵巴拉圭的补时阶段进球后庆祝 [Kirby Lee/Imagn Images]谁可以替代普利西奇？如果普利西奇被排除，波切蒂诺有很多选择来替代他。巴洛贡肯定会在对阵巴拉圭的比赛中梅开二度后带队，这是自伯特·帕特诺德 (Bert Patenaude) 成为 96 年前第一位在世界杯上上演帽子戏法的球员以来，美国国家队球员在世界杯上的首次上演帽子戏法，同样是对阵巴拉圭。三球的胜利追平了他们在世界杯上的最大胜利，同样是在 1930 年，当时美国队在首届比赛中获得第三名。乔瓦尼·雷纳 (Giovanni Reyna) 在对阵巴拉圭的最后阶段上场，打进一记可爱的特里维拉，在补时阶段以 4-1 取得胜利时间 - 但自去年12月以来，他在德国俱乐部门兴格拉德巴赫或他的国家队的比赛中出场时间没有超过60分钟。蒂莫西·维阿在比赛还剩18分钟时替换塞尔吉诺·德斯特后也产生了影响，他有可能插上右路或为巴洛贡提供中路支援，这可能需要麦肯尼切换到进攻左侧。虽然他缺乏同样的X因素，但伯哈尔特在对阵巴拉圭的比赛中替换了普利西奇，并增加了普利西奇缺席的情况下，美国队将错过定位球能力，而上周五留在替补席上的利兹联队勤奋的边锋布伦登·阿伦森或美国俱乐部的亚历杭德罗·赞德哈斯是替代选择，尽管他们都不是波切蒂诺的最爱。波切蒂诺说：“我们正在评估所有可能性，以防万一，然后我们将决定何时得到一个或另一个方向的确认。”周五淘汰赛？是的，事实上他们甚至可以锁定D组头名位置。如果美国队击败澳大利亚队，那么他们就可以以6分保证进入32强赛前两名。但是，一场胜利，再加上后面土耳其队和巴拉圭队的平局，或者巴拉圭队获胜，就可以确保D组第一名。小组赛的名次先以积分决定，然后积分相同的球队之间进行正面交锋，净胜球仅用于区分不同的球队D 组的获胜者将于 7 月 1 日在旧金山湾区体育场对阵 B、E、F、I 或 J 组第三名的球队——可能是波斯尼亚、厄瓜多尔、瑞典、挪威或阿尔及利亚。Nestory Irankunda 是谁？在温哥华的首轮比赛中，Irankunda 创造了历史，27 分钟后，Irankunda 表现出色，成为澳大利亚历史上最年轻的世界杯进球者。这位 20 岁的球员是自 2018 年法国队以来世界杯首发阵容中最年轻的十一人之一，也是第一位在最大舞台上进球的海外出生的澳大利亚人。在他的父母逃离家乡布隆迪的内战后，伊兰昆达出生于坦桑尼亚难民营，伊兰昆达于 2006 年出生，随后作为婴儿搬到了澳大利亚。他在 A 联赛球队阿德莱德联队的青训中成长起来，在第一场比赛中攻入 16 粒进球并贡献 8 次助攻团队，赢得了 sur

articles.title: Get with the times — here's what a 'Luddite' means today
articles.summary: It's often a derogatory term used to describe digital dinosaurs and technophobes. That wasn't always the case. NPR's Word of the Week looks back at the not so backwards-looking Luddites.
articles.titleZh: 与时俱进——这就是“勒德分子”在今天的含义
articles.summaryZh: 它通常是一个贬义词，用来形容数字恐龙和技术恐惧症。情况并非总是如此。美国国家公共广播电台 (NPR) 的《本周热点》回顾了那些不那么落后的勒德分子。

contentParagraphs.en: A protester holds a sign during a protest outside of OpenAI headquarters calling for a pause in AI development in San Francisco on March 21.
contentParagraphs.zh: 3 月 21 日，一名抗议者在旧金山 OpenAI 总部外举行抗议活动，呼吁暂停人工智能开发。

contentParagraphs.en: As artificial intelligence races ahead in the United States, so has the backlash.
contentParagraphs.zh: 随着人工智能在美国的快速发展，人们的强烈反对也随之而来。

contentParagraphs.en: This month, New York moved closer to passing into law a pause on the development of new data centers that power the technology. This graduation season, tech leaders who invoke AI optimism in their commencement speeches are getting booed by classes entering the workforce with anxieties about what AI could mean for their job prospects.
contentParagraphs.zh: 本月，纽约即将通过一项法律，暂停开发为该技术提供动力的新数据中心。今年毕业季，那些在毕业典礼演讲中提及人工智能乐观情绪的科技领袖们，遭到了刚进入劳动力市场的学生的嘘声，他们担心人工智能对他们的就业前景意味着什么。

contentParagraphs.en: When someone dares to question the wonders of technology, there's a handy word used to mock them: Luddites.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当有人敢于质疑技术的奇迹时，有一个方便的词可以用来嘲笑他们：卢德分子。

contentParagraphs.en: David Friedberg, a tech investor and adviser to the White House, recently dropped the term on the popular business podcast All-In: "The idea that AI is going to destroy jobs is a Luddite idea that is being disproven every single day."
contentParagraphs.zh: 科技投资者兼白宫顾问戴维·弗里德伯格 (David Friedberg) 最近在热门商业播客 All-In 上放弃了这个词：“人工智能将摧毁就业机会的想法是卢德分子的想法，每天都在被证明是错误的。”

contentParagraphs.en: Luddites are often accused of being anti-tech and anti-innovation. But Brian Merchant, a tech journalist and self-proclaimed Luddite, says true adherents aren't anti-tech.
contentParagraphs.zh: 勒德分子经常被指责为反科技和反创新。但科技记者、自称勒德分子的布莱恩·麦昌特 (Brian Merchant) 表示，真正的追随者并不是反科技的。

contentParagraphs.en: "The real Luddites are anti-technology being used to exploit people," he said. "A Luddite asks: 'What are the implications of this technology? How is it going to impact society? Should we engage with this technology on the grounds that it might make somebody a lot of money or should we engage with it on the grounds that this could have real impacts for the way that people work and live?' "
contentParagraphs.zh: “真正的勒德分子反对利用技术来剥削人民，”他说。 “一名勒德分子问道：‘这项技术的影响是什么？它将如何影响社会？我们应该因为它可能为某人带来很多钱而参与这项技术，还是因为它可能对人们的工作和生活方式产生真正的影响而参与它？’ ”

contentParagraphs.en: An illustration showing a Luddite leader circa 1812.
contentParagraphs.zh: 插图显示了 1812 年左右的勒德分子领袖。

contentParagraphs.en: Henry Guttmann Collection/Hulton Archive/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 亨利·古特曼收藏/赫尔顿档案馆/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: Henry Guttmann Collection/Hulton Archive/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 亨利·古特曼收藏/赫尔顿档案馆/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: The original Luddites were 19th-century English textile makers who protested the ways mechanization was disrupting their livelihoods by smashing automated power looms. They took their name from a Robin Hood-like folk hero — a disgruntled apprentice worker who historians say likely never existed.
contentParagraphs.zh: 最初的勒德分子是 19 世纪的英国纺织制造商，他们抗议机械化通过摧毁自动化动力织机来扰乱他们的生计。他们的名字取自一位像罗宾汉一样的民间英雄——一位心怀不满的学徒工人，历史学家称他可能从未存在过。

contentParagraphs.en: "It was either a self-descriptor or it was a laudatory term among them that came to be used as a descriptive term by the authorities," said Kevin Binfield, an English professor at Murray State University in Kentucky who edited Writings of the Luddites, an anthology of texts written by the first Luddites and their sympathizers.
contentParagraphs.zh: 肯塔基州默里州立大学的英语教授凯文·宾菲尔德（Kevin Binfield）说：“它要么是一个自我描述，要么是他们中间的一个赞美词，后来被当局用作描述性术语。”他编辑了《勒德分子的著作》，这是一本由第一批勒德分子及其同情者撰写的文本选集。

contentParagraphs.en: How, then, did Luddite become a diss?
contentParagraphs.zh: 那么，勒德分子是如何成为diss的呢？

contentParagraphs.en: For this installment of NPR's Word of the Week, we look at how the Luddites lost the battle — both for their cause and their name's legacy.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在本期 NPR 本周一词中，我们将探讨勒德分子是如何输掉这场战斗的——无论是因为他们的事业还是他们的名字的遗产。

contentParagraphs.en: "By the order of King Ludd"
contentParagraphs.zh: “奉路德国王之命”

contentParagraphs.en: The story starts in early 1800s Nottingham, the heart of England, during a period of industrialization. The arrival of automated power looms and knitting frames threatened to transform the handicraft trade of apprentice clothmakers there.
contentParagraphs.zh: 故事开始于1800年代初期工业化时期的英国中心诺丁汉。自动动力织机和针织机的到来可能会改变当地制衣学徒的手工业贸易。

contentParagraphs.en: These were artisans who developed their skills through years-long apprenticeships before becoming free agents, working in small shops and homes.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这些工匠通过多年的学徒期培养了自己的技能，然后成为自由职业者，在小商店和家庭工作。

contentParagraphs.en: "They had a lot of autonomy," said Merchant, who authored Blood in the Machine, which recounts the history of the 19th century Luddite rebellion. "They could decide how their workday was organized. They would work with their families or sing songs to pass the time, take breaks to make a quick trip to the garden in the most ideal of circumstances."
contentParagraphs.zh: “他们有很大的自主权，”《机器中的血》一书的作者麦昌特说，该书讲述了 19 世纪勒德分子叛乱的历史。 “他们可以决定如何安排自己的工作日。他们会与家人一起工作或唱歌来打发时间，在最理想的情况下休息一下，快速去花园旅行。”

contentParagraphs.en: Big factory owners and bosses changed that. The new machinery — wider, faster looms — allowed for mass production. The workers blamed the bosses for using the new technology to justify lowering prices, driving down quality and reducing wages as they used nonskilled workers to mind the machines, says Merchant.
contentParagraphs.zh: 大工厂主和老板改变了这一点。新机器——更宽、更快的织机——允许大规模生产。麦钱特说，工人们指责老板利用新技术来证明降低价格、降低质量和降低工资的合理性，因为他们使用非技术工人来管理机器。

contentParagraphs.en: "That's what they hated more than anything else. Not the machinery itself, but the way the machinery was being used," he said. "Sort of tearing up the social contract that had governed the way that work was done for so long."
contentParagraphs.zh: “这就是他们最讨厌的。不是机器本身，而是机器的使用方式，”他说。 “这有点撕毁了长期以来支配工作方式的社会契约。”

contentParagraphs.en: The changes triggered the Luddite rebellion, which took place between 1811 and 1817. They started out by waging a peaceful protest campaign. In letters published in the Nottingham newspapers, workers appealed to trade masters to stop their labor practices.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这些变化引发了 1811 年至 1817 年间发生的卢德叛乱。他们首先发动了和平抗议活动。在诺丁汉报纸上发表的信件中，工人们呼吁行业大师停止他们的劳工行为。

contentParagraphs.en: But there were no unions at the time, and without a democratic system to work out their grievances, the workers turned to a more forceful approach. In Nottingham, workers sent threatening letters to employers and broke into factories to destroy the new machines.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但当时没有工会，也没有民主制度来解决他们的不满，工人们转向了更强有力的方式。在诺丁汉，工人们向雇主发出威胁信，并闯入工厂摧毁新机器。

contentParagraphs.en: In writings that appeared in local press, they often undersigned their manifestos with the name of their mythical leader, Ned Ludd.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在当地媒体发表的文章中，他们经常在宣言下方署上他们的神话领袖内德·路德的名字。

contentParagraphs.en: In 1811, The Nottingham Review newspaper recorded what's believed to be the first historical mention of the fictitious Ludd character, described as a framework knitting apprentice near Leicester.
contentParagraphs.zh: 1811 年，《诺丁汉评论》报道了历史上首次提及虚构的路德角色，该角色被描述为莱斯特附近的框架编织学徒。

contentParagraphs.en: "His master was expecting too much of him," Binfield said. "So, what did he do? He went and got a hammer and broke his frame."
contentParagraphs.zh: “他的主人对他的期望太高了，”宾菲尔德说。 “那么，他做了什么？他去拿了一把锤子，打破了他的框架。”

contentParagraphs.en: Ludd's exact motivation was unclear, he says. And there's no evidence he ever existed.
contentParagraphs.zh: 路德说，他的确切动机尚不清楚。而且没有任何证据表明他曾经存在过。

contentParagraphs.en: But it was good branding, says Miriam Cherry, who teaches labor law at New York's St. John's University.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但纽约圣约翰大学劳动法教授米里亚姆·切里 (Miriam Cherry) 表示，这是一个很好的品牌宣传。

contentParagraphs.en: "It was catchy — the idea that there's some fictional general running around in Nottingham Forest who's responsible for the machines getting broken," she said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “这很吸引人——有一个虚构的将军在诺丁汉森林里跑来跑去，他对机器的损坏负有责任，”她说。

contentParagraphs.en: In one 1812 missive that ran in the Review, the framework knitters signed a declaration "by order of King Ludd."
contentParagraphs.zh: 在《评论》刊登的一封 1812 年信件中，框架编织者“奉路德国王之命”签署了一份声明。

contentParagraphs.en: "All the evidence is there was actually no leader," Binfield said. But the idea was that "it would be more threatening to have a mysterious leader."
contentParagraphs.zh: “所有证据都表明，实际上没有领导者，”宾菲尔德说。但他们的想法是“如果有一个神秘的领导者会更具威胁性”。

contentParagraphs.en: As the riots spread outside Nottingham, they grew more violent. The British Army was brought in to restore order, and the state made machine-breaking a crime punishable by death. Several people were hanged. Prosecutors made Luddites out to be misguided vandals, tech writer Merchant says.
contentParagraphs.zh: 随着骚乱在诺丁汉以外蔓延，骚乱变得更加暴力。英国军队被派来恢复秩序，国家将破坏机器定为可判处死刑的罪行。数人被绞死。科技作家麦钱特表示，检察官认定勒德分子是被误导的破坏者。

contentParagraphs.en: "It goes into the books from day one," he said. "They try to associate Luddism with sort of backwards-looking, reactionary tendencies. And it becomes very quickly a useful maneuver for elites and for business interests of every kind to sort of adopt
contentParagraphs.zh: “它从第一天起就被载入史册，”他说。 “他们试图将路德主义与某种落后的、反动的倾向联系起来。这很快就成为精英和各种商业利益采取的一种有用的策略。
